text:
Market today: Chip stocks are under pressure after Samsung's earnings fell short of AI expectations, directly impacting your concentrated tech/chip book. Cyber stocks, however, might be seeing early signs of a comeback.

Your holdings: SOXL (-33.94%) is flagged "SELL" and is a leveraged product facing chip sector headwinds. SPAL (-20.3%) is a new, leveraged ETF already down significantly. PTNM (-15.25%) is a tiny, speculative new position. Remember margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on the broader chip sector's reaction to Samsung's news. Also, watch for follow-through in cyber stocks, as one headline suggests a potential comeback.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged and underperforming positions, especially with sector-specific headwinds emerging.
plays: Your SOXL position is down over 33% and flagged "SELL" amidst chip sector weakness. Seriously consider trimming or exiting this leveraged position to reduce downside risk.
SPAL is a new, leveraged ETF already down 20%; review its protective stop. For PTNM, honor its 9.5 stop to limit further losses on this speculative name.
With headlines pointing to a cyber comeback, watch ZS (Zscaler) for a potential long entry around 153.83-153.59, with a stop at 153.03, as indicated by your scanner.
